controller/resp.go::Resp: The uniform JSON envelope (code, message, timestamp, data) returned by every HTTP handler so clients get a consistent response shape.
controller/resp.go::HandleError: Wraps a generic error into a Resp with the catch-all SYSTEM_ERROR code and the error text, for unexpected failures.
controller/resp.go::HandleCodeError: Builds an error Resp carrying a caller-chosen MessageCode plus the error's message, letting handlers report domain-specific failures.
controller/resp.go::HandleCodeErrorAndMs: Builds an error Resp with a chosen code and a literal message string, used when there is no error object to unwrap.
controller/resp.go::HandleSuccess: Wraps a payload into a Resp with SUCCESS code and "success" message, the standard success return for handlers.
controller/resp.go::HandleInvalidInput: Convenience helper returning an INVALID_PARAMS "invalid input" response, used whenever request binding or validation fails.
controller/resp.go::MessageCode: A string type enumerating the API's namespaced status codes (success, per-domain errors, access-denied, system error) so clients can branch on machine-readable codes.
controller/users.go::UserController: Holds the IUserService dependency and groups the user-facing HTTP handlers (register, login, profile, logout).
controller/users.go::UserController.Regi: Binds the register request JSON, calls the service to create a user, and returns the new user_id; needed to onboard accounts.
controller/users.go::UserController.Logi: Binds login JSON, delegates to the service, and returns the issued auth token, enabling authenticated sessions.
controller/users.go::UserController.GetP: Reads the authenticated userId from context and returns the user's profile, serving the current-user endpoint.
controller/users.go::UserController.Logo: Reads the request's token from context and asks the service to invalidate it, ending the session.
controller/users.go::NewUserController: Constructor injecting the IUserService, wiring the controller during router setup.
controller/balances.go::BalanceControlle: Holds the IBalanceService and exposes the balance-query HTTP handler for the authenticated user.
Reads the authenticated userId from context and returns that user's asset balances, or a QUERY_BALANCE_ERROR on failure.
controller/balances.go::NewBalanceContro: Constructor injecting the IBalanceService when building the controller.
controller/orders.go::OrderController: Holds the IOrderService and groups the order-related HTTP handlers (place, cancel, list).
controller/orders.go::NewOrderController: Constructor injecting the IOrderService into the order controller.
controller/orders.go::OrderController.Pl: Extracts the authenticated user and :market path param, binds the order request, and delegates to the service to place the order, returning the match result.
controller/orders.go::OrderController.Ca: Reads the authenticated userId and :orderId path param and asks the service to cancel that order, returning the cancelled order.
controller/orders.go::OrderController.Ge: Binds pagination/filter query params, injects the authenticated userId, and returns a paginated list of the user's orders.
controller/orderbooks.go::OrderBookContr: Holds the IOrderBookService and exposes the public order-book snapshot handler.
Reads the :market param and returns that market's current order-book snapshot (bids/asks), or a SNAPSHOT_ERROR.
controller/orderbooks.go::NewOrderBookCo: Constructor injecting the IOrderBookService into the order-book controller.
controller/markets.go::MarketDataControl: Holds the IMarketDataService and groups the market-data HTTP handlers (all markets, single market, OHLCV history).
controller/markets.go::NewMarketDataCont: Constructor injecting the IMarketDataService into the market-data controller.
controller/markets.go::MarketDataControl: Returns the ticker/market-data list for every market, powering the markets overview page.
Reads the :market param and returns that single market's data (last price, 24h stats).
Parses market, interval, optional start/end/limit query params (with defaults and bounds), validates the interval, and returns historical candlesticks for charting.
controller/admins.go::AdminController: Holds the IAdminService and groups privileged admin HTTP handlers (manual adjustment, make-market, reset).
controller/admins.go::AdminController.Ma: Binds a SettlementReq and calls the service to manually credit/debit a user's balance, for admin fund adjustments.
controller/admins.go::AdminController.Te: Placeholder handler that currently returns FUNC_NOT_IMPLEMENT; reserved for triggering the auto market maker.
controller/admins.go::AdminController.Re: Wipes all trading state, re-funds demo users, and clears the engine via the service, for resetting the demo exchange.
controller/admins.go::NewAdminController: Constructor injecting the IAdminService into the admin controller.
router.go::setupRouter: Creates the Gin engine, attaches global middleware (CORS, error handler, rate limit), builds every controller from the container, wires routes, and returns the engine.
router.go::setupRoutes: Registers all endpoints: health/metrics, a public group, an auth-protected group behind AuthMiddleware, and an admin group behind AdminMiddleware.
main.go::main: Program entry point: initializes logging, DB, matching engine, and DI container, builds the router and WebSocket server, recovers open orders into the book, starts schedulers, and serves on :8080.
db.go::seedSystemCounters: Loads current orders/trades row counts from the DB into in-memory counters so the UI shows true historical totals from boot.
db.go::initDB: Opens the SQLite DB with WAL/NORMAL/single-connection pragmas for throughput, ensures the OHLCV-1min and credentials tables, purges bad candles, seeds counters, and optionally loads test SQL.
startup_helper.go::initLogger: Creates the log directory and a date-stamped log file, then tees log output to both stdout and that file at the given level.
startup_helper.go::runSQLFilesWithTransa: Runs the schema and testing-data SQL files in order (test mode only) so a fresh DB is rebuilt with mock data on startup.
startup_helper.go::executeSQLFileWithTra: Reads one SQL file, splits it on semicolons, and executes every statement inside a single transaction that rolls back on any error.
startup_helper.go::recoverOrderBook: On boot, reloads each market's NEW/PARTIAL orders and last price from the DB back into the in-memory matching engine so the book survives restarts.
startup_helper.go::convertOrderDTOsToEng: Maps persisted order DTOs to engine model.Order structs, bridging the DB layer to the matching engine during recovery.
startup_helper.go::startUpAllScheduler: Starts all background schedulers (order-book snapshot, market-data, liquidity AMM, WS data feeder), panicking if any fails to launch.
startup_helper.go::setupWebSocket: Spawns a goroutine serving the /ws endpoint on :8081, upgrading connections to the WebSocket hub for real-time feeds.
startup_helper.go::getEthClient: Dials a local Ethereum node at :8545, providing an ethclient for on-chain token operations.
security/credential.go::CredentialCache: A token-to-user cache backed by a credentials DB table so login sessions survive a backend restart via DB rehydration on a miss.
security/credential.go::NewCredentialCac: Constructor initializing the cache with a DB handle and an empty in-memory token map.
Stores a token-to-user mapping in memory and upserts it into the credentials table so the session persists across restarts.
Removes a token from both the in-memory map and the credentials table, used on logout.
Resolves a token to its user from memory, falling back to a DB lookup (rehydrating the cache) after a restart, else returns an invalid-credential error.
middleware/middleware.go::CORS: Returns a permissive CORS middleware allowing all origins, common methods, auth/admin headers, and WebSockets so browser clients can call the API.
middleware/middleware.go::ErrorHandler: Middleware that inspects Gin's accumulated errors after handling and emits an appropriate coded JSON response for public/bind/system error types.
middleware/middleware.go::AuthMiddleware: Validates the Authorization (Bearer) token via the CredentialCache, aborting on failure, and injects user, userId, username, and token into the context.
middleware/middleware.go::AdminMiddlewar: Guards admin routes by requiring the Admin-Token header to equal the hardcoded value (marked TODO), aborting otherwise.
middleware/middleware.go::RateLimitMiddl: Placeholder rate-limit middleware that currently passes all requests through, reserved for future IP-based throttling.
engine-v2/util/deque.go::OrderNodeDeque: A doubly-linked-list deque of order nodes holding one price level's queue, tracking head/tail plus running size (order count) and volume (summed remaining quantity) for O(1) depth reads.
engine-v2/util/deque.go::NewOrderNodeDeq: Constructor returning an empty deque (nil head/tail, zeroed size/volume); a fresh FIFO queue created the first time an order rests at a new price level.
Rests a NEW maker order at the BACK of the queue (newest last); back-insertion is what enforces time priority, and it bumps size/volume. O(1).
Inserts at the FRONT; used to return a partially-filled order to the head so it KEEPS its original early time priority instead of dropping to the back.
Removes and returns the OLDEST order; matching eats liquidity front-to-back, so PopFront is how a fully-filled maker leaves the level. O(1).
Returns the oldest order WITHOUT removing it, letting the matcher test the price-cross and fill amount before deciding to pop or reduce it.
O(1) delete of ANY node (head, tail, or middle) via its embedded prev/next pointers — this is what makes cancelling an order cheap, with no queue scan.
Reports whether the level has zero orders, letting the book side drop an exhausted price level from the price tree.
Returns the O(1) running COUNT of orders at this level (a maintained counter, never recomputed).
Returns the O(1) total remaining QUANTITY resting at this level — the number shown as market depth and consumed during matching.
Returns price × volume: the notional value (in quote currency) resting at this level, used by market-buy-by-quote-amount math.
dto/orders.go::Order: API/persistence DTO for an order carrying id, market, side, sizes, avg price, fees and timestamps; the exchange-facing view of an order distinct from the engine's internal model.Order.
dto/orders.go::Order.MarshalJSON: Custom JSON marshaller that omits price when <=0, formats FeeRate as a percentage string, and emits created/updated timestamps as millisecond epochs for API responses.
dto/orders.go::Order.ToEngineOrder: Converts the DTO into the matching engine's model.Order, copying only the fields the engine needs (id, user, side, price, sizes, mode, fee rate, timestamp).
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder: Fluent builder holding a partially-constructed Order, letting callers assemble an order step-by-step with sensible defaults instead of a large constructor call.
dto/orders.go::NewOrderBuilder: Creates an OrderBuilder pre-seeding a fresh UUID id and current created/updated timestamps so every built order starts uniquely identified and time-stamped.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithMarket: Sets the order's market (e.g. BTC-USDT) on the builder and returns it for chaining.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithUser: Sets the owning user id on the builder and returns it for chaining.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithSide: Sets the order side (bid/ask) on the builder and returns it for chaining.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithType: Sets the order type (limit/market) on the builder and returns it for chaining.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithMode: Sets the order mode (maker/taker) on the builder and returns it for chaining.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithPrice: Sets the limit price on the builder and returns it for chaining; used only for limit orders.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithSize: Sets both OriginalSize and RemainingSize to the given size so a new order starts fully unfilled.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithQuoteAmo: Sets the quote-currency amount on the builder (used for taker bid orders) and returns it for chaining.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.WithFeeRate: Sets both the fee rate and the fee asset on the builder together, returning it for chaining.
dto/orders.go::OrderBuilder.Build: Finalizes the builder by stamping status ORDER_STATUS_NEW and returning the completed Order.
dto/orders.go::PlaceOrderContext: Aggregates all state for one order-placement flow (market, user, request, fee info, order DTO, asset/freeze details, resulting trades) so it can be threaded through service layers.
dto/orders.go::PlaceOrderContext.SyncTra: After the engine matches, syncs the DTO's remaining size and status from the engine order and records the produced trades back into the context.
dto/orders.go::AssetDetails: Holds the base/quote assets plus which asset and how much must be frozen for an order, computed once and reused during placement and settlement.
dto/req_struct.go::RegisterReq: Request body for user registration requiring username and password; validated by binding tags at the controller boundary.
dto/req_struct.go::LoginReq: Request body for login requiring username and password, validated via binding tags before authentication.
dto/req_struct.go::SettlementReq: Admin manual-adjustment request specifying username, asset and a positive amount to credit, validated with gt=0.
dto/req_struct.go::OrderReq: Incoming order request describing side, order type, mode, optional price/size/quote-amount, with binding rules encoding valid combinations (e.g. mode required for limit).
dto/req_struct.go::OrdersQueryType: String type alias distinguishing order-history query categories (opening vs closed) for the orders query endpoint.
dto/req_struct.go::GetOrdersQueryReq: Query-string bound request for paginated order listing (user, market, side, opening/closed type, page size and current page with defaults).
dto/trade.go::PlaceOrderResult: Response returned after placing an order, bundling the resulting order and the list of matches (fills) it produced.
dto/trade.go::Match: Represents a single fill within a place-order result: the executed price, size and timestamp.
dto/trade.go::Match.MarshalJSON: Custom marshaller emitting the match's timestamp as a millisecond epoch integer instead of Go's default time format for consistent API output.
dto/users.go::User: User DTO carrying id, username, VIP level, maker/taker fee rates and creation time; password hash is hidden from JSON via the - tag.
dto/users.go::User.MarshalJSON: Custom marshaller that serializes CreatedAt as a millisecond epoch integer for consistent API timestamp formatting.
dto/balances.go::Balance: Per-asset balance DTO with available/locked/total amounts plus API-only valuation fields (asset value in a quote currency) for display.
dto/markets.go::MarketData: Market ticker DTO exposing a market's name, latest price, 24h price change and 24h volume for market-data endpoints.
dto/resp_struct.go::PaginationResp: Generic paginated response wrapper carrying total count, page metadata, has-next/prev flags and the typed result slice for any listing endpoint.
settings/settings.go::GetAllAssets: Returns the fixed list of supported token symbols; the single source of truth used to seed balances and validate assets across the exchange.
settings/settings.go::ALL_MARKETS: Declares every tradable market with its base/quote assets; used to bootstrap order books and market managers at startup.
settings/settings.go::MAX_QUOTE_AMT_PER_: Per-market cap on quote amount placed at each AMM price level, tuning how much liquidity the automated market maker posts per level.
settings/settings.go::MARGIN_ACCOUNT_ID: Constant account id "0" identifying the system margin account used in settlement/fee bookkeeping.
settings/settings.go::INTERNAL_AMM_ACCOU: Constant account id identifying the internal automated-market-maker account whose orders provide liquidity.
settings/cache_key.go::CacheKeyPrefix: String-based type representing a namespace prefix for cache keys, giving type-safe key construction and avoiding collisions between cache domains.
settings/cache_key.go::CacheKeyPrefix.Ap: Builds a namespaced cache key by joining the prefix and a key with a colon (prefix:key).
Builds a three-part namespaced cache key (prefix:key:suffix) for keys needing an extra qualifier.
settings/cache_key.go::MARKET_DATA_CACHE: Predefined CacheKeyPrefix ("MDC") used to namespace cached market-data entries.
utils/counter.go::IncOrdersPlaced: Atomically increments the process-wide orders-placed counter by one; called by the order service on each placement for system-level metrics.
utils/counter.go::AddTrades: Atomically adds n to the process-wide trades counter, letting one match event contribute its number of fills to the total.
utils/counter.go::GetOrdersPlaced: Atomically reads the current total orders-placed count for display of system totals and per-second rate deltas.
utils/counter.go::GetTradesTotal: Atomically reads the current total trades count for UI system-level totals and rate computation.
utils/counter.go::SetOrdersPlaced: Atomically overwrites the orders-placed counter, used at startup to seed it from the DB so totals reflect full history.
utils/counter.go::SetTradesTotal: Atomically overwrites the trades counter, used at startup to seed it from the DB history.
utils/counter.go::SimTick: Called once per second by the ws feeder; if orders grew since last tick it marks the sim running and accumulates a second, else marks it stopped, inferring run-state from live order flow.
utils/counter.go::SimDuration: Atomically returns the accumulated simulation duration in seconds for broadcasting over the sysstats channel.
utils/counter.go::SimRunning: Returns whether the simulation is currently considered running, based on the flag SimTick maintains.
utils/counter.go::SimReset: Zeroes the sim duration and running flag and rebaselines the last-orders marker so a new simulation run starts cleanly.
utils/math.go::Scale: Constant 1e-7 defining the smallest meaningful float magnitude, used as the precision threshold for money/quantity comparisons.
utils/math.go::RoundFloat: Rounds a float to 7 decimal places to normalize floating-point money/quantity values and avoid tiny representation errors.
utils/math.go::RoundFloats: Rounds every value in a slice to 7 decimals, returning a new slice; convenient for batch-rounding price/volume arrays.
external/index_price_api.go::MarketSumma: Struct mirroring the BTSE market_summary API response so its JSON can be unmarshalled to extract a symbol's last traded price.
external/index_price_api.go::GetIndexPri: Fetches BTSE's market summary over HTTP and returns the last price for the given symbol (erroring if inactive), falling back to 0.01 if not found, supplying an external reference price for the AMM.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::erc20ABI: Minimal ERC-20 ABI JSON containing only balanceOf, enough to encode on-chain token balance queries without the full contract ABI.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::tokenAddresses: Maps token symbols to their Ethereum mainnet contract addresses (currently USDT) for building ERC-20 calls.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::TransferToken: Entry point for on-chain transfers: validates the symbol, derives the sender address from the private key, then dispatches to native-ETH or ERC-20 transfer logic.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::transferETH: Builds, signs (EIP-155, chain id 1337) and broadcasts a native ETH transaction converting the ETH amount to Wei, then prints post-transfer balances.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::transferERC20: Placeholder (TODO) intended to send ERC-20 token transfers; currently unimplemented.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::CheckBalance: Returns whether an address's balance is at least the given amount; for ETH it reads on-chain Wei and compares in Ether, other tokens are stubbed to true.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::QueryBalance: Returns an address's balance as a float; for ETH it reads the on-chain Wei balance and converts to Ether, erroring for unsupported tokens.
chainUtil/chainUtils.go::checkSymbol: Guard that reports whether a symbol is currently supported (only ETH or USDT), used to reject unsupported chain operations early.
engine-v2/model/order.go::Side: An int enum type distinguishing order direction (BID buy vs ASK sell), used everywhere the engine must decide which book side an order belongs to.
engine-v2/model/order.go::Mode: An int enum type marking whether an order is a MAKER (rests on book) or TAKER (matches immediately), driving the limit-order placement branch.
engine-v2/model/order.go::OrderType: An int enum type distinguishing LIMIT from MARKET orders so the order book routes each to the correct matching algorithm.
engine-v2/model/order.go::OrderStatus: A string type enumerating lifecycle states (NEW, PARTIAL, FILLED, CANCELED) so callers and persistence layers can report an order's fill progress.
engine-v2/model/order.go::OrderType.Stri: Returns the human-readable name (LIMIT/MARKET/UNKNOWN) for an OrderType, needed for logging and diagnostics of order routing.
engine-v2/model/order.go::Side.String: Returns the human-readable name (BID/ASK/UNKNOWN) for a Side, needed for readable logs and trade debugging.
engine-v2/model/order.go::Mode.String: Returns the human-readable name (MAKER/TAKER/UNKNOWN) for a Mode, used in log output describing how an order was placed.
engine-v2/model/order.go::Order: The core order struct (ID, user, side, price, original/remaining size, quote amount, mode, fee rate, timestamp) that is the fundamental unit the matching engine stores and matches.
engine-v2/model/order.go::Order.GetStatu: Derives an order's OrderStatus from its size fields (untouched=NEW, near-zero remaining=FILLED, partly consumed=PARTIAL), letting persistence report state without storing it explicitly.
engine-v2/model/order.go::Order.CounterS: Returns the opposite side via Side ^ 1 XOR trick, used to find the book side an incoming order must match against.
engine-v2/model/order.go::NewOrder: Constructor that builds an *Order, initializing RemainingSize to the full size and stamping the current time, ensuring every order starts in a consistent unfilled state.
engine-v2/model/order.go::OrderNode: A doubly-linked-list node wrapping an *Order with Prev/Next pointers, enabling O(1) FIFO queueing and removal within a price level's deque.
engine-v2/model/order.go::NewOrderNode: Constructor that creates an *Order and wraps it in a fresh OrderNode, the convenience entry point for adding a brand-new order to a book side.
engine-v2/model/order.go::OrderNode.Size: Accessor returning the wrapped order's RemainingSize, used by book-side volume accounting and matching to know how much is still available.
engine-v2/model/order.go::OrderNode.Pric: Accessor returning the wrapped order's Price, used by price-level bookkeeping and quote-amount calculations.
engine-v2/market/market.go::MarketInfo: Immutable descriptor of a trading pair holding its name plus base and quote asset symbols (e.g. BTC/USDT), the reference every order book and settlement path consults.
engine-v2/market/market.go::NewMarketInf: Constructor assembling a *MarketInfo from name, base, and quote asset, giving a single well-formed source of pair metadata.
engine-v2/market/market.go::MarketManage: A registry wrapping a map from market name to *MarketInfo, the lookup service the engine uses to validate and resolve markets.
engine-v2/market/market.go::NewManager: Builds a *MarketManager by indexing a slice of markets by name, so subsequent lookups are O(1) at startup.
engine-v2/market/market.go::MarketManage: Returns the names of all registered markets, used to enumerate tradable pairs for APIs and initialization loops.
Looks up a *MarketInfo by name, returning an error if the market is unknown, guarding against orders on invalid pairs.
Convenience wrapper returning a market's (base, quote) asset symbols, used by balance and settlement logic that needs both currencies of a pair.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide: Represents one side (bid or ask) of the book as a price-ordered treemap of price to order deque, plus cached total volume and quote amount for fast top-of-book queries.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::NewBookSide: Constructs a *BookSide with an empty treemap and a flag marking bid vs ask, so best-price selection later picks max or min accordingly.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Ad: Appends a node to its price level's deque (creating the level if absent) and increments cached volume/quote totals, the standard resting-order insert.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Re: Removes a specific node from its price level, deletes the level if it becomes empty, and decrements cached totals, keeping accounting consistent on cancels.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Be: Returns the best price for this side (max for bids, min for asks) using the tree's Max/Min, the core primitive matching relies on.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Pe: Returns the front (oldest) order node at the best price without removing it, letting the matcher inspect the next counterparty before committing.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Po: Removes and returns the oldest node at the best price, cleaning up the empty level and updating totals, the FIFO consume step of matching.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Ha: Reports whether a given price level currently exists, a cheap existence check used before level-specific operations.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Le: Returns the number of distinct price levels on this side, used for depth reporting and diagnostics.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.To: Returns the cached total resting volume on this side, exposing O(1) liquidity depth without walking the tree.
Returns the cached sum of size×price across the side, used by market-bid matching to verify enough quote liquidity exists.
engine-v2/book/book_side.go::BookSide.Pu: Inserts a node at the FRONT of its price level's deque, used to return a partially-filled counterparty order so it keeps time priority.
engine-v2/book/order_index.go::indexEntr: Internal record storing an order's side, price, and node pointer, the value type that lets the index jump straight to an order's location in the book.
engine-v2/book/order_index.go::OrderInde: A map from order ID to indexEntry, providing O(1) lookup so cancels and counterparty removals don't scan the whole book.
engine-v2/book/order_index.go::NewOrderI: Constructor initializing the empty ID-to-entry map for a fresh order book.
engine-v2/book/order_index.go::OrderInde: Registers a node under its order ID, capturing side and price so the order can later be found and removed in constant time.
Deletes and returns the order for a given ID (erroring if absent), the lookup half of cancellation and filled-order cleanup.
Returns an order's side, price, node, and a found flag by ID, enabling CancelOrder to locate the exact book position.
Reports whether an order ID is already indexed, used to reject duplicate order placements before matching.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::Trade: Record of a filled match capturing both sides' order IDs, user IDs, fee rates, price, size, value, and timestamp, the settlement-ready output of matching.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::Trade.Strin: Formats a trade as a readable string (prices/sizes to 8 decimals, fees as percent), used for logging and debugging matches.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::Trade.GetOr: Returns the bid or ask order ID for a given side (panicking on invalid side), letting settlement code fetch the right order per participant.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::PriceVolume: A JSON-tagged price/volume tuple representing one depth level, the unit of an order-book snapshot sent to clients.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::NewPriceVol: Constructor building a *PriceVolumePair from price and volume for snapshot assembly.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::BookSnapsho: A serializable point-in-time view holding top-20 bid/ask levels, latest and best prices, and total sizes, the payload for market-depth APIs and websocket feeds.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::NewBookSnap: Constructor pre-allocating 20-capacity bid/ask slices and a timestamp, the empty snapshot refreshed periodically.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook: Central per-market structure owning bid/ask BookSides, the order index, latest price, a cached snapshot, and RW mutexes; it is where all matching happens.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::NewOrderBoo: Constructs an *OrderBook for a market (panicking on nil market info), wiring up both sides, the index, and an empty snapshot.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.g: Returns the book side matching an order's own side (bid→bidSide), the helper for placing resting orders.
Returns the side an incoming order must match against (bid→askSide), the helper driving taker matching loops.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.S: Returns a deep-copied, mutex-protected copy of the cached snapshot so readers get consistent depth data without racing live mutations.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.c: Deep-copies a slice of price/volume pairs so the returned snapshot shares no pointers with the live book, preventing data races.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.R: Recomputes the top-20 depth, best prices, latest price, and totals under lock, the periodic job that keeps the served snapshot current.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.r: Rebuilds the bid snapshot slice by iterating the price tree from highest downward for up to 20 levels, capturing best-first buy depth.
Rebuilds the ask snapshot slice by iterating the price tree from lowest upward for up to 20 levels, capturing best-first sell depth.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.C: Under write lock, looks up an order in the index, removes it from its book side, and unindexes it, the full cancellation path returning the removed order.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.P: The locked entry point that rejects nil/duplicate orders then dispatches to limit or market placement, the single door for all incoming orders.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.p: Routes a limit order: maker orders rest without matching, taker orders match then rest the remainder and update latest price.
Routes a market order to the bid- or ask-specific market taker by side, then updates the latest traded price.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.m: Adds a resting limit order to its side and registers it in the index without matching, the maker path that provides liquidity.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.t: Matches a limit order against the opposite side while price permits, collecting trades and resting any unfilled remainder, the taker limit algorithm.
Fills a market sell by consuming best bids until the size is met (after checking sufficient volume), returning partially-eaten counterparties to the head.
Fills a market buy by spending a fixed quote amount across best asks (after checking sufficient quote liquidity), computing trade qty from remaining budget.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.T: Returns total ask-side resting volume under read lock, exposing sell-side depth to callers safely.
Returns total ask-side quote amount under read lock, used to gauge sell liquidity in quote terms.
Returns total bid-side resting volume under read lock, exposing buy-side depth safely.
Returns total bid-side quote amount under read lock, the quote-value measure of buy liquidity checked by market-bid matching.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.L: Returns the last traded price under read lock, the reference price for tickers, mark price, and AMM strategies.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.u: Sets latest price from the last trade in a batch (no-op if none), keeping the market's reference price current after each match run.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.r: Thin wrapper delegating to OrderIndex.Remove, centralizing index removal so matching code stays readable.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.a: Thin wrapper delegating to OrderIndex.Add, centralizing index registration for newly rested orders.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.B: Returns the best bid price and total bid volume under read lock, feeding top-of-book quotes and spread computations.
Returns the best ask price and total ask volume under read lock, the sell-side half of top-of-book quoting.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.M: Returns the book's *MarketInfo, letting callers recover the market name and assets from an order book handle.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.G: Returns the market's base and quote asset symbols, used by settlement code to know which balances a trade affects.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.c: Checks price crossability (bid price ≥ best ask, or ask price ≤ best bid), the guard that stops the matching loop when the limit price is no longer satisfied.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.e: Performs one match: pops the best counterparty, computes trade qty, builds the trade, decrements both sizes, and re-heads or unindexes the counterparty.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.c: Assembles a Trade record from two orders and a price/size, normalizing which is bid vs ask so downstream settlement is unambiguous.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.d: Given two matched orders, returns them ordered as (bidOrder, askOrder) so trade creation always labels sides correctly regardless of arrival order.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.P: Locks and rests an order directly via makeLimitOrder without matching, used during order-book recovery to reload open orders from the DB.
engine-v2/book/orderbook.go::OrderBook.U: Locks and sets the latest price to a given value, used during recovery to restore the last known market price from persistence.
engine-v2/core/engine.go::MatchingEngine: The top-level engine holding a mutex, a map of market name to *OrderBook, and the market list, the single facade services call to place/cancel/query orders.
engine-v2/core/engine.go::NewMatchingEng: Constructs the engine (erroring if no markets), pre-creating an order book per market so every tradable pair is ready at startup.
engine-v2/core/engine.go::MatchingEngine: Under write lock, discards and recreates all order books empty, used by the admin reset endpoint to align the engine with cleared DB tables.
Returns the order book for a market or an error if unknown, the internal resolver all order operations funnel through.
Reports whether a market has an order book, a cheap validity check for incoming order requests before deeper processing.
Returns the names of all markets the engine serves, used to enumerate tradable pairs for APIs and schedulers.
Resolves the market's order book, logs the order details, and delegates placement, the engine-level entry for submitting any order.
Resolves the market's order book and delegates cancellation by order ID, the engine-level entry for pulling a resting order.
Returns best bid/ask price and size for a market, a compact top-of-book quote used by tickers and AMM logic.
Rebuilds a market's book on startup by re-inserting persisted open orders and restoring the latest price, so in-memory state survives restarts.
ohlcv/interface.go::TradeStream: Interface abstracting a live trade feed with Subscribe/Close/SyncTrade, letting the aggregator consume trades without knowing the concrete source (in-memory, Kafka, etc.).
ohlcv/interface.go::OHLCVRepository: Interface defining candlestick persistence (save/query bars, realtime upsert, statistics), decoupling the aggregator from the concrete SQLite storage backend.
ohlcv/model.go::OHLCV_INTERVAL: String-typed enum for candlestick timeframes (15m, 1h, 1d, etc.), used as map keys and config lookups to distinguish which bar granularity a trade or query targets.
ohlcv/model.go::MIN_15: Constant OHLCV_INTERVAL("15m") naming the 15-minute candlestick interval so callers reference it symbolically.
ohlcv/model.go::MIN_30: Constant OHLCV_INTERVAL("30m") naming the 30-minute interval (declared though not present in SupportedIntervals).
ohlcv/model.go::H_4: Constant OHLCV_INTERVAL("4h") naming the 4-hour interval (declared but not wired into supported intervals).
ohlcv/model.go::M_1: Constant OHLCV_INTERVAL("1m") naming the 1-minute interval, the finest supported granularity mapped to the ohlcv_1min table.
ohlcv/model.go::Y_1: Constant OHLCV_INTERVAL("1y") naming the 1-year interval (declared but not in supported set).
ohlcv/model.go::Trade: Struct carrying one executed trade (symbol, price, dealt volume, timestamp); the raw input unit the pipeline aggregates into candlestick bars.
ohlcv/model.go::GetOhlcvDataReq: Request struct for querying historical candles (symbol, interval, time range, limit), used by the repository to build the SELECT for chart data.
ohlcv/model.go::OHLCV: Compact column-oriented response struct (parallel arrays s/t/o/h/l/c/v) matching charting-library JSON, the wire format returned to clients.
ohlcv/model.go::OHLCVBar: Internal mutable candlestick holding OHLC prices, volume, quote-volume, open/close times, trade count and closed flag; the in-memory accumulator updated per trade.
ohlcv/model.go::NewOhlcvBar: Constructor initializing a bar with open=high=low=close=openPrice and computing CloseTime from duration, seeding a fresh candle before any trade updates it.
ohlcv/model.go::OHLCVBar.String: Human-readable formatter rendering a bar's fields with formatted times, used for debug/log output.
ohlcv/model.go::OHLCVBar.Update: Applies one validated trade to the bar: bumps high/low, sets close, accumulates volume/quote-volume and trade count; the core per-trade candle-mutation logic.
ohlcv/model.go::OHLCVBar.BatchUpdate: Applies a slice of trades to the bar by looping Update, letting a whole bucket's trades fold into one candle efficiently.
ohlcv/model.go::ohlcvStatistics: Struct holding per-day aggregate stats (record count, time bounds, avg/total volume) persisted to the statistics table for reporting.
ohlcv/config.go::IntervalConfig: Struct pairing an interval's Duration with its DB Table, driving bucket-time math and table routing for each timeframe.
ohlcv/config.go::SupportedIntervals: Map registering the actually-enabled intervals (1min,15min,1h,1d,1w) to their configs; the single source of truth iterated when bucketing trades and closing bars.
ohlcv/config.go::AggregatorConfig: Tunable settings struct (batch size, flush interval, channel size, concurrency, metrics) controlling the aggregator's throughput and buffering behavior.
ohlcv/config.go::AggregatorConfig.Valida: Bounds-checks config fields and defaults MaxConcurrency to 10, rejecting invalid setups before the aggregator starts.
ohlcv/config.go::DefaultAggregatorConfig: Returns a sane default config (batch 100, 1s flush, 1000 channel, 10 workers) used when the caller passes nil.
ohlcv/symbol_bars.go::RealtimeSymbolBars: Per-symbol container mapping each interval to its open bars keyed by bucket open-time, guarded by a RWMutex; holds the live candles being built for one market.
ohlcv/symbol_bars.go::NewRealtimeSymbolB: Constructor that, for every configured interval, seeds a current-bucket bar at the init price so a symbol always has a live candle from the start.
ohlcv/symbol_bars.go::RealtimeSymbolBars: Read-locked lookup returning a copy of the current bucket's bar for an interval, used to serve realtime candle snapshots.
Marks all buckets at/before a target open-time closed, returns them for persistence, deletes them, and seeds the next bar carrying forward close price; the interval rollover mechanism.
Read-locked check whether the symbol tracks a given interval.
Read-locked list of all intervals this symbol maintains, iterated during periodic flush.
Groups trades by (interval,bucket), then under one lock updates existing bars or creates new ones, folding a trade batch into every timeframe's candles at once.
ohlcv/symbol_bars.go::latest: Helper picking the bar with the greater OpenTime (nil-safe), used by CloseBars to know which closed bar seeds the next one.
ohlcv/trade_stream.go::SimpleTradeStream: In-memory TradeStream implementation backed by a buffered channel, feeding trades from the matching engine into the aggregator within one process.
ohlcv/trade_stream.go::NewSimpleTradeStr: Constructor allocating the buffered trade channel of the given size and returning it as a TradeStream.
ohlcv/trade_stream.go::SimpleTradeStream: Records the requested symbols and returns the underlying channel as the receive-only trade feed for the aggregator.
No-op close satisfying the interface since the in-memory stream needs no teardown.
Publishes a trade onto the channel; the producer-side entry point trade settlement calls to push executions into the OHLCV pipeline.
ohlcv/workers.go::WorkerPool: Fixed-size goroutine pool with a task channel and atomic running flag, bounding concurrency of per-symbol bar updates.
ohlcv/workers.go::NewWorkerPool: Constructor allocating a task channel sized at 2x workers and the stop channel, without yet starting goroutines.
ohlcv/workers.go::WorkerPool.Start: Atomically flips to running once and launches the worker goroutines, guarding against double-start.
ohlcv/workers.go::WorkerPool.worker: Long-lived goroutine loop that executes submitted tasks until context or stop channel fires; the pool's execution unit.
ohlcv/workers.go::WorkerPool.Submit: Non-blocking enqueue of a task, returning false if the pool is stopped or its queue is full so callers can shed load.
ohlcv/workers.go::WorkerPool.Stop: Atomically stops once, closes the stop channel, and waits for all workers to drain, ensuring clean shutdown.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator: Central orchestrator wiring repo, trade stream, per-symbol bars, worker pool, config and channels; owns the whole trade to candle to storage pipeline.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::NewOHLCVAggregator: Validates dependencies/config, starts a worker pool, and constructs the aggregator with its trade and stop channels ready.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.Add: Registers a symbol with an initial price and interval configs by creating its RealtimeSymbolBars, rejecting duplicates/empty inputs so the aggregator knows which markets to track.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.Sta: Subscribes to the trade stream and launches the four goroutines (ingest, aggregate, interval timers, periodic flush) that run the pipeline.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.Sto: Closes the stop channel, stops the worker pool, and closes the trade stream for graceful shutdown.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.pro: Goroutine draining the subscription channel into the internal tradeCh, dropping trades (with a warning) if the buffer is full to avoid blocking.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.agg: Goroutine batching incoming trades and flushing the batch when it fills or the flush ticker fires, balancing latency against per-trade overhead.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.pro: Groups a batch by symbol and submits each symbol's trades to the worker pool for concurrent UpdateByTrades, fanning out candle updates.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.man: Spawns one timer goroutine per supported interval to drive bar closing on their respective boundaries.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.sta: Per-interval minute-ticker loop that detects when a bucket boundary has passed and triggers closeIntervalBars, tracking the last closed timestamp to avoid duplicates.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.fin: Computes whether the previous bucket has fully elapsed and is newer than the last closed one, returning its open-time or 0; the boundary-detection math.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.clo: Ranges over all symbols, closes their bars for the given interval/open-time, and upserts the finished candles to the repository.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.per: Goroutine firing every minute to flush in-progress realtime bars to storage so open candles are persisted between closes.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.flu: Ranges over all symbols/intervals writing the current open bar via UpdateRealtimeOHLCV, keeping the realtime table in sync with memory.
ohlcv/aggregator.go::OHLCVAggregator.Get: Public historical-candle query that clamps the limit (default 500, max 1000) and delegates to the repository.
Returns the current in-memory bar for a symbol/interval straight from the memory cache, erroring if absent, for low-latency live reads.
Wraps GetRealtimeOHLCV into the client-facing OHLCV array format for a single current candle.
ohlcv/repository.go::SQLiteOHLCVReposito: SQLite-backed OHLCVRepository implementation holding the *sql.DB, the concrete persistence layer for candles.
ohlcv/repository.go::NewSQLiteOHLCVRepos: Constructor injecting the database handle into the repository.
ohlcv/repository.go::SQLiteOHLCVReposito: Inserts-or-replaces a single bar into its interval's table, routing by interval and mapping the closed flag to an int.
Builds a filtered SELECT (symbol/time-range, closed bars only, ordered, limited) and assembles rows into the columnar OHLCV response for charts.
Insert-or-replaces the current open bar into the shared ohlcv_realtime table so in-progress candles are queryable.
Fetches one realtime bar by symbol/interval/open-time, attaching duration and returning nil on no-rows.
Insert-or-replaces per-day aggregate stats keyed by symbol/interval/date into the statistics table.
Transactional batch upsert that, per bar, either inserts or accumulates (high=max, low=min, close/volume/count merged) so re-closed buckets fold correctly; the core historical-write path.
Resolves an interval to its table name via SupportedIntervals, erroring on unsupported intervals; central table routing.
Looks up an interval's time.Duration from the config map, used to set a fetched bar's duration.
Converts a bool to 1/0 for SQLite's integer is_closed column.
Transactionally promotes one realtime bar into its history table as closed and deletes it from the realtime table; atomic finalize of a candle.
Reads back per-day aggregate stats by symbol/interval/date, returning nil on no-rows.
ohlcv/utils.go::getBucketUnixTime: Truncates a trade time to its interval boundary and returns the Unix open-time, the bucket key used to group trades into candles.
ohlcv/utils.go::getBucketTime: Returns the interval-aligned bucket start as a time.Time, used by the interval timer's boundary math.
ohlcv/utils.go::getNextBucketTime: Advances one interval and truncates, giving the next bucket's start time.
ohlcv/utils.go::getNextBucketUnixTime: Next-bucket start as Unix seconds, used when seeding the follow-on bar after closing the previous one.
repository/impl/orders.go::orderReposito: Empty struct implementing IOrderRepository; a stateless receiver whose methods take an injected DBExecutor, so the same code runs on a raw DB or a transaction.
repository/impl/orders.go::NewOrderRepos: Constructor returning an orderRepository as the IOrderRepository interface, enabling dependency injection and decoupling callers from the concrete type.
repository/impl/orders.go::orderReposito: Runs an INSERT writing all 17 order columns; needed to persist a newly placed order and wraps any driver error with context.
Updates an order's remaining_size, status and updated_at by id, guarding correctness by erroring when RowsAffected is 0 (order not found).
Fetches a single order by id via QueryRowContext, translating sql.ErrNoRows into a clear not-found error; needed for lookups before cancel/settle.
Returns all of a user's orders matching one status, newest first; used to list a user's open or historical orders.
Like the single-status query but builds a dynamic IN (?) placeholder list so callers can fetch several statuses at once (e.g. all open states) safely parameterized.

Atomically applies a fill to an order in one UPDATE — decrements remaining size, adds quote amount, recomputes avg price, sets FILLED/PARTIAL via a CASE, and adds fees; core of trade settlement persistence.
Sets an order's remaining_size and marks it CANCELED by id, erroring if no row matched; needed to persist cancellation atomically.
Overwrites an order's original_size (e.g. after market-order sizing adjustments), erroring when the order id is absent.
Fetches every order in a market matching a dynamic status set; used by market-wide views and the AMM/liquidity logic to see resting orders.
Combines user, market and a dynamic status IN filter, letting a user view their orders scoped to one market.
Builds a dynamic filtered query (user, statuses, optional market/side/time), runs a COUNT plus a LIMIT/OFFSET page, and returns a populated PaginationResp; powers paged order history.
Counts orders in ('NEW','PARTIAL') for a market (taking a raw *sql.DB); used by metrics/AMM to gauge live book depth per market.
repository/impl/balances.go::balanceRepo: Stateless struct implementing IBalanceRepository; its methods take an injected DBExecutor so balance mutations can run inside the caller's transaction.
repository/impl/balances.go::NewBalanceR: Constructor returning a balanceRepository as IBalanceRepository for dependency injection.
repository/impl/balances.go::balanceRepo: Loads a user's per-asset available/locked rows and computes Total in Go; needed to show wallet balances.
Increments or decrements available balance by sign; the decrease branch guards funds with available >= ? so an over-draw affects 0 rows and returns an insufficient-funds error.
Increments or decrements locked balance by sign; the decrease path uses locked >= ? to atomically prevent unlocking more than is held.
Atomically moves funds from available to locked in one UPDATE gated by available >= ?, so order placement can reserve funds without a check-then-act race.
Atomically moves locked funds back to available using MAX(0, locked - ?); needed to release reservations on cancel/settle without going negative.
Bulk-inserts zero-valued balance rows for a set of assets in one multi-VALUES statement; used at registration to seed a new user's wallet efficiently.
Applies signed deltas to both available and locked in one UPDATE; the general-purpose settlement primitive for crediting/debiting a user's asset.
repository/impl/users.go::userRepository: Stateless struct implementing IUserRepository, with methods taking a DBExecutor so user reads/writes participate in the caller's transaction.
repository/impl/users.go::NewUserReposit: Constructor returning a userRepository as IUserRepository for dependency injection.
repository/impl/users.go::userRepository: Fetches a user (including fee tiers) by id, mapping sql.ErrNoRows to a not-found error; used for authorization and fee lookups.
Fetches a user by username for login/registration checks, returning a clear not-found error when absent.
Inserts a new user row with id, credentials and default fee fields; needed to persist a registration.
Updates a user's password_hash by id, erroring when no row matches; used for password changes.
Updates a user's VIP level and maker/taker fee rates together, erroring if the user id is absent; keeps fee tier consistent with VIP status.
repository/impl/trades.go::tradeReposito: Stateless struct implementing ITradeRepository, its methods taking a DBExecutor so trade writes/reads run inside the settlement transaction.
repository/impl/trades.go::NewTradeRepos: Constructor returning a tradeRepository as ITradeRepository for dependency injection.
repository/impl/trades.go::tradeReposito: Persists all trades from a match in a single multi-VALUES INSERT, returning early on empty input; efficient trade-history recording during settlement.
Returns the most recent trade price for a market (latest timestamp), erroring when no trades exist; feeds last-price and market-data displays.
Returns the last trade price at or before a given time, used to compute 24h-ago price for percent-change calculations.
Sums trade size within a time window using COALESCE(SUM(size),0) so empty ranges return 0; provides 24h volume for market data.
repository/interface.go::DBExecutor: Abstraction over the three context DB methods shared by *sql.DB and *sql.Tx, letting every repository method run identically inside or outside a transaction.
repository/interface.go::IUserRepository: Contract for user persistence (get/insert/update-pwd/update-vip), decoupling services from the concrete SQL implementation for injection and testing.
repository/interface.go::IBalanceReposit: Contract for balance persistence including atomic lock/unlock and signed-modify operations, the data-layer boundary the money logic depends on.
repository/interface.go::IOrderRepositor: Contract for order persistence and queries (insert/update/cancel/sync-fill/paginate/count), abstracting order storage from services.
repository/interface.go::ITradeRepositor: Contract for trade persistence and market-stat reads (batch insert, latest price, price-ago, volume), decoupling settlement and market-data code from SQL.
service/interface.go::ICacheService: Interface for a generic in-memory key/value cache (Update/Get), used to memoize computed market data.
service/interface.go::IUserService: Business-layer contract for user lifecycle — get, register, login (returns a token) and logout — abstracting auth from controllers.
service/interface.go::IOrderBookService: Contract exposing order-book reads (snapshot, latest price, base/quote assets) so controllers and other services query the engine without touching it directly.
service/interface.go::IBalanceService: Contract for reading a user's balances, keeping balance-fetch logic behind a service boundary.
service/interface.go::IOrderService: Central trading contract — place/cancel orders plus varied query and pagination methods — defining the exchange's order operations for controllers.
service/interface.go::IAdminService: Contract for privileged operations (manual settlement, test market-making, full exchange reset) used by admin endpoints.
service/interface.go::IAutoMarketMakerSe: Contract with BootUp to start automated liquidity provision across the given markets.
service/interface.go::IPriceIndexService: Empty marker interface reserved for an external price-index service abstraction (no methods yet).
service/interface.go::IMarketDataService: Contract for market statistics — compute/get market data, list all, and fetch OHLCV history — powering ticker and chart endpoints.
service/impl/txn.go::WithTx: Runs fn inside a DB transaction, committing on success and rolling back on error or panic (re-panicking after rollback); the reusable atomicity wrapper for all multi-step money operations.
service/serviceHelper/settlement.go::Ord: Struct capturing the per-order deltas (remaining-size decrease, dealt-quote increase, fee increase) needed to apply a fill, decoupling settlement computation from DB writes.
service/serviceHelper/settlement.go::Use: Struct accumulating a user's net base/quote available and locked changes across all trades, so each user gets one consolidated balance update.
service/serviceHelper/settlement.go::Tra: Aggregate holding all order updates, per-user settlements, dealt totals and accumulated base/quote fees; the computed plan a settlement transaction applies.
service/serviceHelper/settlement.go::Pro: Pure function that turns a placed order plus its trades into a full TradeSettlementResult, computing per-trade balance moves, order updates and the eaten order's avg price before any DB write.
service/serviceHelper/settlement.go::ini: Creates an empty UserSettlementData for every user involved in the trades, so subsequent per-trade logic can accumulate into existing map entries.
service/serviceHelper/settlement.go::ext: Collects the unique bid and ask user IDs across all trades into a set, used to size and seed the settlement map.
service/serviceHelper/settlement.go::Tra: Processes one trade: tallies dealt amount/size and delegates to the bid- and ask-side balance handlers plus the opposite-order update, the per-trade heart of settlement.
Computes the bid user's balance changes — unlocking quote (refunding overpayment on limit buys), deducting base fees, crediting received base — and returns the base-asset fee.
Computes the ask user's balance changes — releasing locked base, deducting quote fees, crediting received quote — and returns the quote-asset fee.
Records the fill update (size, quote amount, fee) for the resting counter-order, picking ask or bid order id and fee based on the incoming order's side.
Records the incoming (eaten) order's own fill update, special-casing market-buy orders (already sized) and adding accumulated fees to it.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::ParseM: Looks up a market's order book from the engine and returns its base/quote assets, validating non-nil engine and non-empty market; a shared prerequisite for freeze/refund/fee logic.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::Determ: Decides which asset and how much to lock when placing an order — base+size for asks, or delegating to the bid calculation — so funds are reserved correctly before matching.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::calcul: Computes the quote-asset amount to freeze for a buy: price*size for limit orders or the requested quote amount for market orders.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::NewLim: Builds a limit-order dto.Order from the placement context via the builder, centralizing limit-order construction with fee info.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::NewMar: Builds a market-order dto.Order (price -1, taker mode) from context, using quote amount for buys and size for sells.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::NewEng: Converts a persistence-layer dto.Order into the matching engine's model.Order, bridging the DTO and engine domains (nil-safe).
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::Calcul: Computes the asset and amount to unlock when cancelling — price*remaining quote for bids, remaining base for asks — so leftover reserved funds are returned correctly.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::WrapPl: Packages the resulting order and its trades into a PlaceOrderResult API response, mapping trades to lightweight Match entries.
service/serviceHelper/helpers.go::Determ: Resolves the fee rate (maker vs taker from the user's tier) and the fee asset (base for bids, quote for asks), panicking on unknown mode/side; drives per-order fee charging.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService: The concrete order-service struct holding DB, matching engine, and order/trade/balance repositories plus the kline trade stream, wiring persistence to the in-memory engine.
service/impl/orders.go::NewIOrderService: Constructor injecting DB, engine, repos, and trade stream, returning the IOrderService interface so callers depend on the abstraction, not the impl.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.Pla: Top-level order entry: builds context, picks limit/market strategy, executes it, bumps system order/trade counters, and wraps the result for the API.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.ini: Validates the request, parses market into base/quote assets, computes freeze amount and fee info, and packages them into a PlaceOrderContext.
service/impl/orders.go::OrderPlacementSt: Interface abstracting order-placement behavior so limit and market orders share one execution flow via distinct strategy implementations.
service/impl/orders.go::LimitOrderStrate: Strategy struct implementing limit-order placement, building a limit order DTO and running placement in non-market mode.
