service/impl/orders.go::MarketOrderStrat: Strategy struct implementing market-order placement, building a market order DTO and running placement in market mode.
Constructs the market-order DTO from the request then delegates to executeOrderPlacement with isMarketOrder=true.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.get: Maps an OrderType (LIMIT/MARKET) to its strategy, returning ErrInvalidInput for unknown types.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.exe: Two-phase orchestrator: runs the transactional placement phase, then the trade-settlement phase, logging and normalizing errors.
In one DB transaction: locks funds, inserts the order, submits it to the engine, syncs trade results, batch-inserts trades, streams klines, and fixes market-bid original size.
Post-match settlement: computes settlement from trades, syncs matched order updates, updates each user's balances, and settles fee revenue.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.upd: Writes a user's post-trade base and quote asset available/locked balances, needed to reflect matched trades in persistent balances.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.Can: Validates ownership and open status, cancels in the engine, then transactionally updates the order and refunds the unlocked frozen balance.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.Que: Lists a user's orders filtered to open or closed statuses depending on the flag, for the my-orders view.
Like QueryOrder but scoped to a single market, used by both users and the AMM proxy to fetch open orders per market.
Fetches all orders for a market matching given statuses, a lower-level query used for market-wide order inspection.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.Pag: Paginated order history: maps opening/closed query type to statuses (closed limited to last 3 months) and delegates to the repo.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.set: Credits collected base and quote trading fees into the exchange's margin account, capturing exchange revenue from each match.
service/impl/orders.go::getOrderStatuses: Helper returning the NEW/PARTIAL statuses for open orders or CANCELED/FILLED for closed, centralizing the open/closed status mapping.
service/impl/orders.go::validatePlacingO: Guards order placement by rejecting nil user/request, empty market, and zero/undersized size, price, or quote-amount per side and type.
service/impl/orders.go::orderService.Cou: Returns the count of open orders in a market, used by the metrics service to gauge live book depth.
service/impl/markets.go::MarketDataServi: Struct providing 24h market statistics (last price, change, volume), backed by the trade repo, an in-memory cache, and the OHLCV aggregator.
Returns cached MarketData for every configured market, reading precomputed values from the cache to serve the markets list cheaply.
Fetches one market's cached MarketData, returning an error if the market's data is absent from the cache.
Delegates candlestick history requests to the OHLCV aggregator, exposing historical klines through the market data service.
service/impl/markets.go::NewMarketDataSe: Constructor wiring DB, trade repo, cache, and OHLCV aggregator, returning the IMarketDataService interface.
service/impl/markets.go::MarketDataServi: Computes fresh 24h stats (latest price, 24h-ago price, percentage change, volume) from trade data, used by the scheduler to refresh the cache.
Helper querying the trade repo for the market's most recent traded price.
Helper querying the trade repo for the market's traded price as of 24 hours ago, used to compute price change.
Helper querying the trade repo for total traded volume over the last 24 hours.
service/impl/users.go::userService: Concrete user-service struct holding DB, user/balance repos, and the in-memory credential (token) cache.
service/impl/users.go::NewIUserService: Constructor injecting DB, repos, and credential cache, returning the IUserService interface.
service/impl/users.go::userService.GetUs: Fetches a user profile by ID via the user repo, backing the get-profile endpoint.
service/impl/users.go::userService.Regis: Transactionally creates a user with a generated UID and bcrypt-hashed password, seeds all-asset balances, and grants a 500 USDT starting balance.
service/impl/users.go::userService.Login: Verifies username/password with bcrypt, then issues a UUID token stored in the credential cache and returns it.
service/impl/users.go::userService.Logou: Deletes the session token from the credential cache, invalidating the user's authenticated session.
service/impl/users.go::genUIDSecure: Generates a human-readable user ID of form UID{yymmdd}{4-letters}{4-digits} using crypto/rand for uniqueness.
service/impl/orderbooks.go::orderBookSer: Thin struct wrapping the matching engine to expose read-only order-book queries.
service/impl/orderbooks.go::NewIOrderBoo: Constructor injecting the matching engine, returning the IOrderBookService interface.
service/impl/orderbooks.go::orderBookSer: Returns a point-in-time bid/ask depth snapshot of a market's book from the engine, for API and WebSocket order-book feeds.
Returns the market's latest traded price from the engine (-1 on error), used as an index-price fallback and for valuations.
Resolves a market name into its base and quote asset symbols via the engine's market info.
service/impl/admins.go::adminService: Admin operations struct holding DB, user/balance repos, the order service, and the engine for privileged exchange management.
service/impl/admins.go::NewIAdminService: Constructor injecting DB, repos, order service, and engine, returning the IAdminService interface.
service/impl/admins.go::adminService.Res: Wipes orders/trades/OHLCV, zeroes and re-funds demo user balances, and resets the in-memory engine and counters without a restart, so tokens/WebSockets stay valid.
service/impl/admins.go::adminService.Set: Admin manual balance adjustment: credits a named user's asset available balance by the requested amount inside a transaction.
service/impl/admins.go::adminService.Tes: Seeds a test ETH-USDT book by placing five bid and five ask maker limit orders as a fake market_maker, for demoing/testing.
service/impl/caches.go::CacheService: Thread-safe in-memory key/value store (RWMutex-guarded map) used for caching computed market data.
service/impl/caches.go::NewCacheService: Constructor initializing the backing map and returning the ICacheService interface.
service/impl/caches.go::CacheService.Upd: Writes a value under a key with a write lock, used by schedulers to publish freshly computed market data.
service/impl/caches.go::CacheService.Get: Reads a value by key under a read lock, returning the value and a found flag for cache-miss handling.
service/impl/balances.go::balanceService: Struct providing user balance listings with USDT valuations, backed by DB, user/balance repos, and the market data service.
service/impl/balances.go::NewIBalanceSer: Constructor injecting DB, repos, and market data service, returning the IBalanceService interface.
service/impl/balances.go::balanceService: Fetches a user's balances and enriches each with a USDT valuation (1:1 for USDT, else total times latest market price) for portfolio display.
service/impl/metrics/metrics.go::init: Package init that registers all Prometheus gauge vectors so they are exported on the metrics endpoint at startup.
service/impl/metrics/metrics.go::MetricS: Struct that periodically samples order-book, order-service, and scheduler-reporter data to update Prometheus gauges.
service/impl/metrics/metrics.go::NewMetr: Constructor injecting the order-book service, order service, and scheduler reporter for the metric collector.
service/impl/metrics/metrics.go::MetricS: Single tick entry point that refreshes order-book, open-orders, and scheduler-execution metrics together.
Per market, reads a book snapshot and sets bid/ask total-volume and latest-price gauges for monitoring depth and price.
Per market, counts open orders and sets the open-orders-count gauge for observability of live book activity.
Reads the scheduler reporter and sets a per-job execution-count gauge, surfacing background-job health.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::Bal: AMM-local value type holding base/quote asset symbols and their available/locked amounts, decoupling market-making logic from balance DTOs.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::New: Constructor building a Balance from explicit base/quote symbols and available/locked figures.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::IAm: Interface exposing the exchange operations an AMM strategy needs (balance, index price, book snapshot, open orders, place/cancel), abstracting the underlying services.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::Amm: Concrete proxy implementing IAmmExchangeFuncProxy over the order-book, balance, order, and user services plus an HTTP client for index prices.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::New: Constructor injecting the four services and HTTP client, returning the IAmmExchangeFuncProxy interface.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::Amm: Fetches the AMM account's balances and projects them into a per-market Balance of base/quote available and locked amounts.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::Mar: Struct mapping the BTSE market_summary API JSON, used to parse external index-price data for the AMM.
service/impl/amm/strategy_helper.go::Amm: Fetches the symbol's last price from the BTSE API, falling back to the local latest price (or 0.01) when unavailable, giving the AMM a mid-price anchor.
Returns a dereferenced book snapshot for a market via the order-book service, exposing current depth to the strategy.
Returns the AMM account's open orders in a market by delegating to the order service's per-market open query.
Places an order on behalf of the AMM user via the order service, discarding the result and returning only the error.
Cancels an AMM order by ID via the order service, used when repositioning maker quotes.
service/impl/amm/provide_liquidity_amm.g: Market-making strategy struct holding the exchange proxy and AMM user/UID, providing two-sided liquidity around an index price.
Constructor building the strategy from the exchange proxy and AMM user, returning the AutoMarketStrategy interface.
Value type pairing a price with a target volume, representing one desired maker quote level in the ladder.
Core cycle: fetches index price, balance, and open orders, computes ideal bid/ask ladders, and reconciles the live book toward them.
Builds a 20-level bid or ask ladder spaced +/-0.1% off the index price, sizing each level by available balance capped at the per-level quote limit.
Classifies existing open orders into bids and asks, then reconciles each side against its ideal ladder.
For one side, cancels orders whose price is off-ladder or whose size drifts over 10%, then places new orders at missing ideal levels.
Places a single maker limit order at a given price/volume/side through the exchange proxy, logging the buy/sell action.
Rounds a price to 2/3/4 decimals based on magnitude, keeping quotes on sensible tick sizes.
Rounds an order volume to 3 or 4 decimals depending on size, normalizing quote quantities.
service/impl/amm/amm_strategies.go::Auto: Interface every AMM strategy implements, exposing MakeMarket so the scheduler can drive any strategy uniformly.
service/impl/amm/amm_strategies.go::Stra: Named int type enumerating AMM strategy kinds (provide-liquidity, grid, arbitrage) for selection.
service/impl/amm/amm_strategies.go::GetS: Factory returning the AutoMarketStrategy for a given Strategy id (currently only PROVIDE_LIQUIDITY), erroring on unknown ones.
ws/model.go::WSChannel: A string-typed enum for WebSocket feed channel names; needed so subscribe/broadcast logic can switch on a small closed set of channel identifiers rather than raw strings.
ws/model.go::OHLCV: Channel constant "ohlcv" for candlestick/K-line feeds; lets clients subscribe to per-symbol/interval price candles.
ws/model.go::ORDERBOOK: Channel constant "orderbook" for live bid/ask depth snapshots of a market; drives the real-time order book display.
ws/model.go::MARKETS: Channel constant "markets" for the market-summary list feed (all markets' latest price/change/volume); powers the markets overview.
ws/model.go::USER_DATA: Channel constant "user_data", a private per-user feed of open orders, trade history and balances, requiring a token to identify the user.
ws/model.go::SYSSTATS: Channel constant "sysstats", a public system-wide counters feed (total orders/trades, sim state) so the live badge updates without login.
ws/model.go::WSAction: String-typed enum for request intent (subscribe/unsubscribe); lets the client dispatch inbound requests to the correct handler.
ws/model.go::WSReq: Inbound WebSocket request struct carrying Action, Channel and free-form Params; the wire contract clients send to subscribe or unsubscribe.
ws/model.go::OHLCVReqParams: Params struct for an OHLCV subscription (Symbol + Interval); identifies which candle stream the client wants.
ws/model.go::OrderBookReqParams: Params struct for an orderbook subscription (Market); identifies which market's depth feed to stream.
ws/model.go::PrivateReqParams: Params struct for the private user_data subscription carrying a Token (to resolve the user) and Market (to scope orders).
ws/model.go::WSResp: Outbound response envelope (Channel, Data payload, Timestamp) marshalled to each subscriber so clients know which feed a message belongs to.
ws/model.go::SubscriptionKey: Composite key of Channel plus its typed Params, used as the map key that groups clients subscribed to the exact same feed.
ws/model.go::BuildSubscriptionKey: Converts a raw WSReq into a canonical, typed SubscriptionKey by re-decoding Params per channel; needed so identical subscriptions hash equally and unknown channels are rejected.
ws/model.go::WSFeedPackage: Pairs a SubscriptionKey with a Data payload; a transport unit for pushing feed data to the hub's broadcast machinery.
ws/client.go::Client: Represents one connected WebSocket client with its connection, buffered Send channel, subscription set, hub pointer and mutex; the per-connection state the hub manages.
ws/client.go::NewClient: Constructor that builds a Client with a 1024-deep buffered Send channel (headroom against brief stalls) and an empty subscription map.
ws/client.go::Client.ReadPump: Goroutine loop that reads inbound frames, enforces read limits/deadlines and pong-based keepalive, decodes each into a WSReq, and dispatches it; unregisters the client on disconnect.
ws/client.go::Client.handleRequest: Routes a decoded WSReq to subscribe or unsubscribe handling based on its Action; central inbound dispatcher.
ws/client.go::Client.handleSubscribe: Builds the SubscriptionKey from the request and registers the client with the hub for that feed.
ws/client.go::Client.handleUnsubscribe: Builds the SubscriptionKey and removes the client from that feed's subscriber set via the hub.
ws/client.go::Client.WritePump: Goroutine loop that drains the Send channel to the socket with write deadlines, and periodically (54s) sends ping frames to keep the connection alive and detect dead peers.
ws/client.go::HandleWebSocket: HTTP handler that upgrades a request to a WebSocket, creates a Client with a unique ID, registers it with the hub, and starts its read/write pump goroutines.
ws/client.go::DataFeeder: Interface (Start + Feed) abstracting a component that pushes feed packages into the WS system; lets the hub accept pluggable/mock data sources.
ws/hub.go::Hub: Central manager holding all clients, register/unregister/broadcast channels and the subscriptions map; the single goroutine-safe coordinator of the WebSocket fan-out.
ws/hub.go::upgrader: Package-level websocket.Upgrader with a permissive CheckOrigin; needed to upgrade HTTP connections to WebSocket (origin check is a TODO for prod).
ws/hub.go::NewHub: Constructor initializing the Hub's client map, channels and subscriptions map so it is ready to Run.
ws/hub.go::Hub.GetSubscriptionKeys: Returns a snapshot slice of all currently-subscribed keys under a read lock; used by the feeder to know which feeds to collect and push.
ws/hub.go::Hub.Run: The hub's event loop: serializes register, unregister (with subscription cleanup + channel close), and broadcast events on a single goroutine, exiting on context cancel.
ws/hub.go::Hub.Subscribe: Adds a client to a subscription key's set (creating it if absent) and records the key on the client, both under locks, so future broadcasts reach it.
ws/hub.go::Hub.Unsubscribe: Removes a client from a key's subscriber set (deleting the empty set) and from the client's own subscription map under locks.
ws/hub.go::Hub.BroadcastToSubscribers: Wraps data in a WSResp, marshals it, and non-blockingly sends to every client subscribed to that key, unregistering any whose send buffer is full (slow/dead client).
ws/mock.go::MockDataFeeder: Empty struct acting as a test/demo data source that emits fabricated OHLCV and orderbook frames instead of real market data.
ws/mock.go::Feed: A stubbed (TODO) package function intended to feed a WSFeedPackage; currently a no-op placeholder.
ws/mock.go::MockDataFeeder.Start: Runs two tickers (5s OHLCV, 2s orderbook) that broadcast hardcoded BTC-USDT sample candles and depth to subscribers until the context is cancelled; used to exercise the WS pipeline without a live engine.
scheduler/market_job.go::MarketDataSched: Periodic job that recomputes per-market summary data (price/change/volume) and refreshes the cache; holds the data + cache services, ticker, market list and a run counter.
scheduler/market_job.go::NewMarketDataSc: Constructor that derives the market-name list from settings.ALL_MARKETS and wires the data/cache services and tick duration into a MarketDataScheduler.
scheduler/market_job.go::MarketDataSched: Returns the job's identifier "MarketDataScheduler" for reporting/metrics.
Returns the mutex-guarded execution count, letting the reporter/metrics observe how many times the job has run.
Atomically (mutex) increments the run counter each cycle; keeps RunTimes accurate under concurrent reads.
Runs an immediate update then launches a ticker goroutine that recomputes market data every duration until stopped; satisfies the Scheduler contract.
Stops the ticker and closes the stop channel to end the background loop, logging the halt.
Per cycle, recomputes each market's data via the data service and writes it under the market's cache key; the actual work that keeps cached market summaries fresh.
scheduler/orderbook_snapshot_job.go::ord: Periodic job holding the matching engine and duration that refreshes each market's in-memory order book snapshot; run-count guarded by a mutex.
scheduler/orderbook_snapshot_job.go::New: Constructor wiring the MatchingEngine and tick duration into an orderBookSnapshotScheduler returned as a Scheduler.
scheduler/orderbook_snapshot_job.go::ord: Returns the job name "orderBookSnapshot" for reporting.
Returns the mutex-guarded run count for reporting (note: the Start loop does not currently increment it).
Mutex-guarded increment of the run counter (defined but not invoked in the current Start loop).
Launches a ticker goroutine that, each tick, iterates every market's order book and calls RefreshSnapshot so served depth snapshots stay current.
No-op stop implementation satisfying the Scheduler interface (returns nil without tearing down the goroutine).
scheduler/lqdt_amm_job.go::LQDTScheduler: Automated market-maker job that periodically provides liquidity across markets on behalf of the internal AMM account; holds the AMM proxy, duration, AMM user and run counter.
Returns the job name "AMM" for reporting.
Returns the mutex-guarded run count so the reporter can track AMM cycles.
Mutex-guarded increment of the run counter, called each market-making cycle.
scheduler/lqdt_amm_job.go::NewLQDTSchedu: Constructor that fetches the internal AMM account (fatal on failure) via the user service and wires the AMM exchange proxy and duration into an LQDTScheduler.
scheduler/lqdt_amm_job.go::LQDTScheduler: Builds the provide-liquidity strategy and launches a ticker goroutine that, each cycle, calls MakeMarket for every market using its per-level max quote amount (default 1 USDT).
Unimplemented stop that panics; the AMM job is not designed to be gracefully stopped in the current code.
scheduler/ws_data_feeder.go::WSDataFeede: Scheduler that pushes live data into the WebSocket hub, aggregating the OHLCV, orderbook, market, sysstats and private user-data services needed to build each channel's payload.
scheduler/ws_data_feeder.go::NewWSDataFe: Constructor injecting the hub plus all data sources (OHLCV aggregator, orderbook/market/order/balance services, credential cache) into a WSDataFeederJob.
scheduler/ws_data_feeder.go::WSDataFeede: Launches two ticker goroutines: a 1s loop (advances sim clock, feeds all subscriptions) and a 400ms fast loop for orderbook only; keeps clients updated without REST polling.

For a given subscription key, fetches the appropriate data (sysstats counters, market list, realtime OHLCV, orderbook snapshot, or private orders/balances) and broadcasts it to that key's subscribers.
Unimplemented stop that panics; the feeder job has no graceful-shutdown path currently.
Returns the job name "WSDataFeederJob" for reporting.
Returns 0 (this job does not track run counts); present only to satisfy the Scheduler interface.
scheduler/scheduler_reporter.go::Schedul: Aggregates a list of schedulers and collects their run-time counts; the bridge feeding scheduler execution metrics to the metrics service.
scheduler/scheduler_reporter.go::NewSche: Constructor storing the slice of schedulers to be reported on.
scheduler/scheduler_reporter.go::Schedul: Small struct pairing a job's Name with its execution Times; the per-job unit returned by Report.
Iterates all registered schedulers, logs and collects each job's name and run count into a slice of reports for metrics consumption.
scheduler/interfaces.go::Scheduler: The common interface (Start, Stop, Name, RunTimes) all background jobs implement, so the container and reporter can manage them uniformly.
container/container.go::Container: The application's dependency-injection root holding every constructed dependency (DB, repositories, services, cache, engine, schedulers, metrics, AMM proxy, OHLCV aggregator, WS hub) in one place.
container/container.go::NewContainer: Builds the whole object graph in dependency order (cache, repos, OHLCV, services, WS, proxy, schedulers, metrics), returning a fully wired Container; the single composition root of the app.
container/container.go::Container.initRe: Instantiates the user/balance/order/trade and SQLite OHLCV repositories onto the container.
container/container.go::Container.initSe: Constructs and wires all business services (user, order, orderbook, admin, cache, market-data, balance) with their required repos, engine and dependencies.
container/container.go::Container.Cleanu: Closes the database connection (logging errors) on shutdown to release resources.
container/container.go::Container.initSc: Constructs the market-data, orderbook-snapshot, AMM and WS-feeder schedulers with their intervals and registers the first three with a SchedulerReporter for metrics.
container/container.go::Container.initPr: Builds the AMM exchange-function proxy that lets the AMM strategy place/cancel orders and read balances via the internal services (with a 30s HTTP client).
container/container.go::Container.initMe: Creates the MetricService wired to the orderbook/order services and scheduler reporter so runtime metrics can be exported.
container/container.go::Container.initOH: Sets up the OHLCV trade stream and aggregator, seeds each market's first candle price via the external index API (falling back to last-traded or 65000 when blocked), and starts aggregation.
container/container.go::Container.initWS: Creates the WebSocket Hub and launches its Run event loop in a background goroutine so clients can connect and receive feeds.
